breakClass: left-join null member access / non-nullable value-column read on a possibly-null DefaultIfEmpty() entity in a server-side projection (EF Core 10 shaper reads SQL NULL into a non-nullable CLR value type â€” decimal/int/DateTime/enum â€” and throws; EF6 silently produced default(T))
why: Lines 734-741 read non-nullable b.Quantity/b.Price and c.InvoiceNo/c.AssetEntryDate without the (decimal?)/(DateTime?) casts used in the sibling projections at 573-575/653-657. If the asset-transfer detail/master left join is empty, these read NULL into non-nullable -> 500. Server-side. Note the same controller's other two projections DO cast to nullable (safe) â€” this third one regressed by omission. MED.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED port-break in QuickNetCore/Controllers/AssetFromInventoryController.cs, method GetAccountWiseReport (lines 715-746), projection at 732-743 (the cited line 738 NetQty = b.Quantity - ConvertedQty, plus 734 invoice = c.InvoiceNo, 739 Price = b.Price, 741 AssetEntryDate = c.AssetEntryDate).

(a) SERVER-SIDE: The query is one IQueryable composed entirely from db.Items joined via DefaultIfEmpty() to db.AssetTransferDetails (b), db.AssetTransferMasters (c), db.Accountss (d), db.ItemUnits (e). There is NO .ToList()/.AsEnumerable()/.AsAsyncEnumerable() boundary before the projection. The only materialization is .ToList() at line 745. Therefore the select-new at 732-743, the .Where(o => o.NetQty > 0), .Distinct(), .OrderBy() (744) and .Count() (746) all translate to SQL. Confirmed server-side.

(b) WOULD 500: b (AssetTransferDetail) and c (AssetTransferMasters) are left-joined, so an Item with no asset-transfer detail yields a NULL b/c row. The projection reads non-nullable value-type members directly: per Models/Asset.cs, AssetTransferDetail.Quantity = decimal (line 23), .Price = decimal (line 25); AssetTransferMasters.InvoiceNo = long (line 43), .AssetEntryDate = DateTime (line 47). EF Core 10's shaper reads SQL NULL into these non-nullable CLR structs and throws "Nullable object must have a value". (ConvertedQty at 724 is already guarded with ?? 0, but b.Quantity in NetQty = b.Quantity - ConvertedQty is NOT, so the crash happens during the read, before the NetQty>0 filter can exclude the row.) db.Items realistically contains items with no asset transfer detail, so this fires on realistic data, not just edge data. Severity HIGH (hunter said MED; I upgrade because unmatched Items are the norm, not the exception).

PORT REGRESSION vs legacy: _archive/legacy-mvc5/Controllers/AssetFromInventoryController.cs GetAccountWiseReport (lines 758-786) is structurally identical â€” same direct projection of the same non-nullable members of the same DefaultIfEmpty() entities â€” and worked under EF6 (EF6 shaper produced default(T) for NULL rows: invoice=0, Price=0, AssetEntryDate=0001-01-01, NetQty=0-ConvertedQty which is then filtered out by NetQty>0). So the EF Core 10 behavior is a regression by omission.

REGRESSED BY OMISSION relative to its own siblings: the same controller's other two reports were already fixed for this exact bug with explicit comments â€” GetAccountWiseReportMOve (lines 634-673) and the GetItemWiseReport-area query (lines 552-599) both use the materialize-entity-only-as-nullable-then-reproject-client-side pattern and cast (decimal?)b.Quantity / (decimal?)b.Price / (long?)c.InvoiceNo / (DateTime?)c.AssetEntryDate. GetAccountWiseReport is the single projection that was missed. Not a group-aggregate over an IGrouping; not a standalone top-level FirstOrDefault; not post-materialization; not commented out. All triage exclusions ruled out.
